name: Phyllo Tarts with Fresh Berries and Creme Fraiche
recipeIngredient: Cream for Creme Fraiche: 1 1/2 cups whipping cream, 1/3 cup granulated sugar, 1/3 cup yogurt, 2 tbsp lemon juice
Butter for Phyllo Tarts: 1/3 cup butter
Cream Filling: 1/2 cup whipping cream, 2 tbsp granulated sugar, 1 tsp vanilla
Raspberry Coulis: 7.5 oz frozen raspberries, 2 tbsp lemon juice (optional sugar)
Mixed Berries: 3 cups assorted berries (raspberries, blackberries, blueberries, strawberries - sliced)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make Creme Fraiche: In a small bowl, combine whipping cream, granulated sugar, yogurt, and lemon juice until blended. Cover loosely with cheesecloth or kitchen cloth and let stand at room temperature until thickened for about 24 hours. Drain the liquid through a sieve lined with cheesecloth. Use the thickened creme fraiche as directed.
2. Prepare Phyllo Shell Tarts: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Melt butter over low heat. Lay phyllo sheets on a counter, covered with wax paper and damp cloth. Brush one sheet with melted butter, then another, placing them in the muffin cups to form tart shells. Press into the bottom, keeping edges upright. Prick bottoms with a fork.
3. Bake for 8-10 minutes until crisp and brown. Remove from muffins, cool on rack. Store at room temperature; do not refrigerate.
4. Prepare Cream Filling: In an electric mixer bowl, beat whipping cream, granulated sugar, and vanilla until soft peaks form. Gently fold in the Creme Fraiche. Refrigerate until ready.
5. Make Raspberry Coulis: Blend raspberries with lemon juice in a blender or processor until smooth. Strain to remove seeds. Adjust sweetness if needed; refrigerate.
6. Assemble Tarts: Before serving, spoon creme fraiche filling into each shell. Top with mixed berries and sprinkle icing sugar. Pour raspberry coulis over plates. Garnish with mint sprigs if desired. Serve immediately.
Makes 12 tarts
